pages.raw_text:
The indications are now begin.
ming to manifest themselves
around the city hall that the par.
ties to the building of the Monroe
street bridge, the city, the w. w.
P. and the Cowles cohorts, have
agreed that the bridge should be
built on these terms:


The w. w. P. will waive it's
claim to perpetual franchise and
pay 100000 toward the cost of
the bridge, taking a franchise for
possibly 50 years; the Cowles in
terests will withdraw all further
opposition to the contentious of
the w. w. p, and the city will
waive all further claim against
the w. w. P. for bridge repairs,
and foot the balance of the bill
for building the bridge.


Some understanding along this
line is about to be forced through
the city council SO that there will
be no further dispute about the
Monroe street bridge when cleo
ton day comes around.
